tool.name: BEAST2
tool.command: mkdir -p results && ln -s '$input_xml' 'results/beast_input.xml' && cd results && beast -threads "\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1}" -overwrite -statefile 'beast_input.xml.state' #if str($definitions).strip() -D '$definitions' #end if #if str($loglevel) -loglevel $loglevel #end if #if str($run_mode.mode) == 'validate' -validate #else #if str($run_mode.seed) -seed $run_mode.seed #end if #if str($run_mode.errors) -errors $run_mode.errors #end if $run_mode.sampleFromPrior #end if 'beast_input.xml' #if str($run_mode.mode) == 'validate' > '$validation_report' #end if

param.name: input_xml
param.label: BEAST2 XML input file
param.help: BEAST 2 analysis specification, typically produced by BEAUti. This must be a BEAST 2 XML, not a BEAST 1 XML.

param.name: mode
param.label: Run mode
param.option: Validate XML only (parse, do not run)

param.label: Random number seed
param.help: Set for reproducibility. Leave blank for a random seed.

param.label: Sample from prior
param.help: Ignore the data likelihood and sample from the prior only.

param.label: Maximum numerical errors
param.help: Number of numerical errors tolerated before stopping.

param.name: definitions
param.label: Attribute-value definitions
param.help: Comma-separated key=value pairs substituted into the XML, e.g. chainLength=1000000,tree=mytree

validator.message: Only alphanumeric characters, '.', '_', '-', '=', ',' and spaces are allowed

tool.help:
**What it does**
BEAST 2 (Bayesian Evolutionary Analysis Sampling Trees) is a cross-platform program for Bayesian phylogenetic analysis of molecular sequences using MCMC. It is oriented towards rooted, time-measured phylogenies inferred under strict or relaxed molecular clock models, and is used for divergence-time estimation and testing evolutionary hypotheses.
-----
**Input**
- **BEAST2 XML file**: A complete BEAST 2 analysis specification (sequence data, tree prior, substitution model, MCMC settings), typically generated with BEAUti. It must be a **BEAST 2** XML - BEAST 1 XML files are not compatible.
-----
**Options**
- **Run mode**: Run the full MCMC analysis, or only validate (parse) the XML without running. - **Random number seed**: Set a specific seed for reproducibility. - **Sample from prior**: Sample from the prior distribution instead of the posterior. - **Maximum numerical errors**: Number of numerical errors tolerated before stopping. - **Log level**: Verbosity of BEAST's screen logging. - **Attribute-value definitions (-D)**: Comma-separated ``key=value`` pairs substituted into the XML at runtime, e.g. ``chainLength=1000000``.
-----
**Output**
BEAST writes output files whose names are defined inside the input XML via ``<logger fileName="...">`` elements, and an analysis can define any number of them. Outputs are therefore returned as collections:
- **Trace logs** (tabular): parameter estimates and likelihoods sampled during the MCMC run. - **Tree logs** (beast.trees): sampled trees in NEXUS format.
An XML that defines only a screen logger (no file loggers) will produce empty collections.
-----
